reasoning:
The baseline average of 128.25 protests corresponds to approximately 32 protests per week. Given the typical right-skew of event data, a flat trend would normally give a <50% chance of an arbitrary 30-day period exceeding the mean. However, protest activity in Israel over the 360 days preceding October 2025 likely followed an upward trajectory as public pressure regarding hostages and early elections intensified. Therefore, recent and upcoming months are expected to systematically exceed the 12-month average. 

For the 2025-11-02 period, the 30-day window encompasses key two-year anniversary dates of the October 7 attacks, which serve as major focal points for mass mobilization and memorial protests, overriding the usual dampening effect of the High Holidays. 
For 2025-11-25, weekly protests will resume their normal cadence without holiday interruptions, keeping the rate elevated. 
For 2026-01-24, greater uncertainty applies over the longer horizon, and typical deep-winter weather (rain) in January generally reduces the geographic spread and frequency of protests in Israel. Thus, the probability naturally regresses toward 0.50.
